acceptance: Different plan revisions produce different artifact paths and leave the first plan bytes and digest unchanged.
A semantic constraint replacement creates a new policy directory and leaves prior definition and plan artifacts unchanged.
Changed bytes at an existing artifact path fail without mutation.
Same bytes at the same artifact path converge idempotently.
Implementation, changed-path, review, verification, and delivery artifacts use the current policy and plan revision directory.
Delivery reads the revision-specific changed-path manifest rather than a fixed filename.
Cancelled, completed, and replayed workflows resolve historical artifact references without path rewriting.
Traversal, absolute, oversized, and malformed relative paths continue to fail closed.
excluded: No approval-gate redesign or behavior change from Increment A.
No migration, rewrite, copy, or deletion of existing profile-local evidence or historical artifact references.
No mutable current symlink, directory scan, or inferred current revision.
No live controller, profile, board, cron, gateway, worktree, GitHub-ready, issue closure, installation, push, release, or deployment mutation.
No retention or commit of the candidate without separate approval.
authorization: Approval authorizes only repository-local Increment B candidate implementation and verification within the exact paths and exclusions. It does not authorize retention, commit, installation, live probing, issue closure, push, release, deployment, or cron resume.
